genre_refs: nostalgic suburban Americana; warm autumnal small-town aesthetic
description: A Department of Labor designed slogan graphic. The background is a warm, sun-dappled photograph of an idyllic suburban street in autumn: a cream/light-colored two-story house with a porch, a white picket fence, golden fall foliage on overhanging trees, a manicured lawn and hedges, and a brick path. An American flag hangs from the house and, at right, a parent walks hand-in-hand with a small child down the path, evoking family and home. A small circular DOL/eagle seal sits at center. Across the middle in white italic script is the slogan 'Recapture the Dream.' The accompanying tweet declares 'The American Dream has been stolen from the American People' by 'decades of failed policies' that 'prioritized foreign labor' and 'offshored our jobs,' vowing under POTUS to 'restore the American Dream for EVERY AMERICAN.' This is one of a recurring DOL 'Recapture the Dream' series pairing idealized white-picket Americana imagery with economic-nationalist messaging; the technique uses nostalgia to imply a lost golden age to be reclaimed, aimed at the administration's populist working-class base.
